category_title: Children with ASD exhibiting disruptive sensory-seeking and out-of-seat behaviors
minimal_context: Matthew is transitioning from a reinforcing activity to his desk to begin his morning academic targets.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Matthew, your reinforcing activity is finished. It is time to sit at your desk for our morning teaching session.

STUDENT: (Puts down the activity but does not walk toward his desk, standing in the transition area instead.)

TUTOR: Let's go to your seat to start your academic targets.

STUDENT: (Turns away from the desk area and begins walking in the opposite direction of his assigned seat.)

TUTOR: Matthew, you need to come back to your chair.

STUDENT: (Continues walking around the room, leaving his seat without permission and not returning to his seat for the teaching time.)

options.A: Implements a traffic light sticker system for the morning teaching session, awarding a green sticker for 100% in-seat behavior, an orange sticker for under ten minutes out of his seat, and a red sticker for over ten minutes.
options.B: Uses humor by joking a lot and laughing at themselves during the transition to encourage the student to return to his assigned seat for the morning teaching session.
options.C: Uses a 'request for clarification' during the transition, asking the student for explanations and elaborations regarding why he is walking away from his assigned seat for the morning teaching session.
options.D: Reads poems aimed at promoting reflection and the reinforcement of mindfulness concepts during the transition to encourage the student to return to his assigned seat for the morning teaching session.
